name: The Kent Law Firm
description_short: Atlanta-based bankruptcy law firm specializing in Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 filings, debt relief, and estate planning with 40+ years of experience.
description_long:
The Kent Law Firm is a father-and-son bankruptcy practice based in Atlanta, Georgia, operating for over 40 years. The firm focuses exclusively on personal bankruptcy and will preparation, positioning itself as a debt relief agency that helps clients file for bankruptcy relief under the Bankruptcy Code. Their practice emphasizes personalized client relationships and accessibility throughout the bankruptcy process.

The firm offers Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 bankruptcy filings, creditor harassment cessation, foreclosure and repossession prevention, garnishment and lien resolution, and will preparation services. They provide free initial consultations and advertise reasonable fees compared to competitors. The firm explicitly targets seniors, military veterans, and government workers with specialized cost-effective legal services. They also address bankruptcy's intersection with divorce proceedings and provide credit rebuilding guidance post-filing.

The Kent Law Firm differentiates itself through availability and personalized service. They offer Saturday appointments, commit to attorney presence throughout cases (criticizing competitors who don't appear in court), emphasize responsive communication, and promise to treat clients like family. The messaging focuses on emotional support alongside legal expertise, acknowledging client shame and stress while positioning the firm as a "hand up, not a hand out."

The firm's scope is narrow but focused. As a law firm, they cannot offer debt settlement, consolidation, or credit repair services—they specifically handle bankruptcy filings and related legal matters. Their positioning around relationship-building and accessibility is credible but typical of local law practices. The 40-year history and father-son structure suggest stability, though individual attorney credentials are not detailed on the homepage.

Consumers considering bankruptcy should also explore alternatives. Debt relief programs may negotiate settlements for less than owed, while debt consolidation loans can simplify payments. Credit counseling agencies offer free financial assessments. After bankruptcy, rebuilding credit through secured credit cards and credit builder loans provides a structured path back. Credit repair services can help ensure accurate reporting. After discharge, qualifying for an installment loan can begin rebuilding payment history on your credit report.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Over 40 years of combined bankruptcy and estate planning experience
Free initial consultations with no obligation to file
Saturday appointments available for working clients
Father-and-son team structure suggests continuity and established reputation
Explicit commitment to attorney presence in court and client accessibility
Specialized discounted services for seniors, military veterans, and government workers
Addresses multiple debt relief tools (Chapter 7, Chapter 13, foreclosure prevention, garnishment)
cons: Website lacks individual attorney credentials, bar admission details, or specific case results/testimonials
No transparent fee structure provided—only vague mention of 'reasonable fees'
Limited geographic service area (Atlanta/Georgia focus) despite national bankruptcy law applicability
No information about response time expectations or case timeline management
Cannot offer debt settlement or consolidation services—limited to bankruptcy filings only
best_for: Georgia residents facing Chapter 7 or Chapter 13 bankruptcy filing
Seniors and military veterans seeking cost-effective bankruptcy representation
Individuals needing foreclosure prevention or creditor harassment cessation
People with estate planning needs concurrent with bankruptcy matters
diagnosis: The Kent Law Firm is best for Georgia residents needing bankruptcy filing representation, particularly those valuing personal attorney availability and specialized support for vulnerable populations. The main caveat is that they offer legal representation only—not debt consolidation, settlement, or credit repair—so clients seeking non-bankruptcy debt solutions should seek other providers.
services: Creditor harassment cessation and cease-and-desist assistance
Specialized services for seniors and military veterans
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
